claimText: Dumping occurs when a company exports a product at a price significantly lower than its domestic market price.
evidenceExcerpt: Dumping occurs when a company exports a product at a significantly lower price than its domestic market price.
examUse: Statement-based question on the definition of international trade practices.
distractorHooks: Defining dumping as selling below the cost of production rather than below the domestic market price
Confusing dumping with currency manipulation or export subsidies

claimText: An anti-dumping duty is a protectionist tariff imposed on foreign imports that are dumped in the domestic market.
evidenceExcerpt: An anti-dumping duty is a protectionist tariff imposed on foreign imports dumped in the domestic market.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question distinguishing between different types of trade remedies.
distractorHooks: Confusing anti-dumping duty with countervailing duty, which targets subsidized imports
Stating anti-dumping duty is a purely revenue-generating tariff rather than a protectionist measure

claimText: The General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT) permits countries to take action against dumping.
evidenceExcerpt: The General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT) permits countries to take action against dumping.
examUse: Statement-based question on WTO/GATT provisions regarding trade remedies and protectionist tariffs.
distractorHooks: Claiming GATT strictly prohibits any form of anti-dumping duties because they are protectionist
Attributing the permission to regulate dumping to a different international body like the IMF or World Bank
